Provide a summary of the changes you've made here. If this PR fixes a specific issue, please link it (e.g., `Fixes #123`, `Closes #456`), these keywords will automatically close the linked issue when the PR is merged.

## AI Code and Agentic Tools
**ETS2LA does not allow agentic tools in our codebase.** This rule does not affect third party plugins. Ask mode is allowed for debugging etc... code generated by ask mode is not allowed (implement changes yourself). If you understand this, please remove this section before submitting your PR. Failure to do so will result in your PR being rejected. We retain the right to reject any PR that violates this rule, even if the section has been removed.

## Type of change
Check the relevant options *(place an "x" between the brackets)*
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would break functionality, e.g. a function name change)
- [ ] This change requires a documentation update on https://docs.ets2la.com

## Checklist
- [ ] I have written this code myself, with AI assistance limited to inline autocompletion and ask mode.
- [ ] I can explain why I made the changes I did, and how they work.
- [ ] If I used code from another source, I have cited it in the comments and explained how/if I adapted it to fit the codebase. (e.g. `Originally from StackOverflowLink, adapted to fit our codebase by ...`)
- [ ] My code is documented with C# standard comments explaining public methods and variables (`/// <summary>...</summary>`) and any complex logic (if applicable). Less comments is better, so if you find yourself writing lots of comments, consider refactoring to be more self-explanatory.
- [ ] This PR changes only the code necessary to achieve the desired functionality. This PR does not edit code in unrelated namespaces. If you have multiple features, split those into separate branches and PRs. Exceptions are made for breaking changes, where changing code in multiple namespaces is often necessary.
